usage: Sal Ammoniac, Sal Armeniac, Regenerated Salt, Niter, Salt of Tartar, Soda Ash, Plant Alkali, Borax, White Vitriol
Blue Vitriol, Green Vitriol, Atrament, Cinnabar, Red Lead, Litharge, White Lead, Crocus of Iron, Crocus of Copper

🝀🝔.name: Operation of Alchemy
usage: Crucible, Sublime, Precipitate, Calcine, Dissolve, Coagulate, Digest, Ferment, Fix, Separate, Conjoin, Cerate
Reverberate, Albification, Rubification, Fixation (2), Circulation, Solution, Fusion, Extraction, Projection

🝕🝣.name: Process of Alchemy
usage: Putrefy, Incerate, Imbibe, Wet-Separate, Cleanse, Wash, Strain, Filter,
Evaporate, Cohobate, Distil, Slow-Digest, Melt, Liquefy, Congeal, Pulverize

🝤🝳.name: Essence of Alchemy

Aqua Fortis, Aqua Regia, Aqua Vitae, Balneum Maris, Balneum Roris
Oleum Sulphuris, Oleum Vitrioli, Oleum Tartari, Oleum Philosophorum, Essence of Gold

systemic fails.market crash: a cascade of panic unravels investor confidence
systemic fails.stack overflow: a recursive push decrements pointer to no return
systemic fails.traction loss: passing slip ratio replaces tire road dialogue with noise
systemic fails.thermal runaway: exothermic reaction featuring a rapid escalation
systemic fails.rolling blackout: sequential loss of power propagating across the network
systemic fails.alpine avalanche: fracture propagates, gravity pries snow slab from the granite
systemic fails.acoustic feedback: escalating audio oscillation from excess self amplification

fundament.star: Guidance, aspiration, directional; north star, lodestar.
fundament.axis: Orientation, structure, alignment; pole, axis mundi, unit vector.
fundament.flow: Movement, process, temporal reference; epoch, equilibrium, par.

orient.azimuth: Horizontal angle measured clockwise from north; specifies directional bearings in navigation.
orient.zenith: Point on celestial sphere directly above observer; defines upward vertical reference.
orient.nadir: Point on celestial sphere directly below observer; defines downward vertical reference.
orient.home: Baseline reference position or starting point; anchors all relative coordinate measurements.
orient.datum: Reference surface or framework in surveying; establishes baseline heights and positions.
orient.origin: Point where all coordinate axes intersect; absolute reference for all other points.
orient.fovea: Central point of retina providing sharpest vision; primary reference for visual fixation.
orient.frame: Baseline reference for measuring motion or orientation; inertial, conceptual, or structural.
orient.anchor: Initial information used as reference point for subsequent judgments and estimates.
orient.tonic: First note of diatonic scale; home note and primary harmonic reference for a key.
orient.equilibrium: State of balance in a system; reference point for dynamic stability.
orient.par: Standard score or expected performance; baseline reference for comparisons.
orient.mean: Arithmetic average of a dataset; central reference for statistical deviation.
orient.bedrock: Foundational layer or principle; ultimate reference for stability or truth.
orient.pole: Extreme point defining an axis or boundary; reference for orientation.
orient.epoch: Reference point in time for dating events or cycles; standard temporal baseline.
orient.sovereign: Supreme authority or ruling power; ultimate reference for legitimacy and control.
orient.absolute: Unrestricted and final authority; reference beyond conditions or limits.
orient.axiom: Self-evident truth taken as starting point; foundational reference for reasoning.
orient.principle: Fundamental rule or law; baseline reference for systems of thought or action.
orient.archetype: Original model or pattern; reference form for subsequent instances.
orient.monolith: Single massive structure; reference of permanence or authority.
orient.root node: Primary or topmost node in hierarchical structure; defines fundamental reference for descendants.
orient.north star: Fixed celestial point above North Pole; constant navigational reference for true north.
orient.prime meridian: Meridian at 0° longitude; universal reference for measuring east-west position.
orient.unit vector: Vector of length 1 indicating direction; fundamental reference for specifying other vectors.
orient.ground state: Lowest possible energy level of quantum system; baseline reference for all other states.
orient.identity element: Element that leaves operation result unmodified; fundamental operational reference.
orient.sea level: Average height of the ocean's surface; standard baseline for measuring land elevations and depths.
orient.axis mundi: Singular point connecting realms; central reference for orientation or structure.
